organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Reichman University (previously IDC Herzliya), the first private institution of higher education in Israel, opened its doors in 1994 thanks to the vision of Prof. Uriel Reichman. Reichman University's ten schools offer innovative, dynamic undergraduate and graduate programs that educate Israel's future business, juridical, high-tech, communications, economics, and civic leaders. 
All of the academic programs provide students with both a solid theoretical background and the practical interdisciplinary skills that are essential for success in the 21st century. 

The university’s alumni are leading key positions at government agencies, high-tech companies, and financial institutions. 

Reichman University is unique in its educational methods, which are based on an interdisciplinary approach and the teaching of information technology and global markets. It combines theory with real-world experience and provides its students with proficiencies. 

Our basic outlook, which is rooted in the twin concepts of individual freedom and responsibility, emphasizes student entrepreneurship and leadership alongside a commitment to community service.

Follow us on all social media channels >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

מטרת אוניברסיטת רייכמן (לשעבר הבינתחומי) היא להכשיר את מנהיגות העתיד של מדינת ישראל, באמצעות השכלה בינתחומית, המשלבת ידע תיאורטי מעמיק עם הכשרה מעשית, ובהנחיית סגל מרצים וחוקרים מהאוניברסיטאות המובילות בעולם ובישראל. גישתנו היא הקפדה על מצוינות במחקר ובהוראה, לצד דגש על אחריות חברתית ומעורבות בקהילה. מאז הקמתו, הצליח הבינתחומי הרצליה להיות חלוץ בתחומים רבים ולהוביל מהפכה במערכת ההשכלה הגבוהה בישראל, שאת עקבותיה ניתן לראות כיום במוסדות אקדמיים אחרים.

עקבו אחרינו בכל הרשתות החברתיות >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

Serving millions of customers worldwide with TurboTax, QuickBooks, Credit Karma and Mailchimp, we believe that everyone should have the opportunity to prosper and we work tirelessly to find new, innovative ways to deliver on this belief.

• At Decido, we are building the future of online marketing technology.
• As part of my role as General Manager, I am leading Decido's technological and business growth strategy, driving technology innovation and operational excellence.

• As a key executive at Perion (NASDAQ: PERI), I successfully led the technology, product and operations of the publishing monetization technology unit.
• Strategic Leadership: Led the technology, product, and operations, resulting in significant market leadership and business growth.
• Technology & Innovation: Directed the development of cutting-edge monetization technologies leveraging ML and AI algorithms, to build innovative solutions, positioning Perion as a leader in the industry.
• Operational Success: Streamlined operations and cultivated high-performing, autonomous development teams that consistently delivered innovative technology solutions.

• The Jerusalem Post group is a media and content publisher group with more than 1500 employees.
• Technology Leadership: I successfully oversaw all digital and technological aspects of the business, driving increased monetization revenue and increase in organic traffic, through innovative solutions.
• Platform Development: Managed the development of top-notch content and publishing platforms, for web, mobile and smart TVs, ensuring they met the business needs of the entire Jerusalem Post group.
• Vendor Management: Successfully managed third-party vendors and suppliers, optimizing resources and maintaining high-quality outputs.

• As an executive director at USAGC, I led the company's R&D and technology strategy.
• I managed the company's R&D, engineering and IT groups.
• Strategic Vision: Defined and executed the company’s technological vision and strategy, driving growth through custom technological solutions.
• R&D Management: Led and scaled the R&D team, delivering advanced technological solutions, including websites, mobile apps, and an in-house CRM platform.
• Budget Management: Oversaw annual budget planning, ensuring cost-effective delivery of technology projects.

• As an R&D consultant for Oracle within the IDF 8200 unit, I played a key role in establishing the architecture and technological solutions for one of the largest data integration and collection projects in the IDF.
• Architectural Leadership: Provided critical R&D consultation for one of the IDF’s largest data integration projects, improving efficiency and system uptime.
• Process Optimization: Enhanced project management processes and facilitated seamless technology integration between Oracle and Microsoft systems.

• As a department manager, I oversaw the entire development lifecycle of the unit.
• I led the development of the unit's large-scale projects, including innovative solutions for data integration, business intelligence and fraud detection algorithms.
• I managed a group of over 25 engineers (Developers, QA, and Analysts), delivering projects on time and within budget.
• I successfully implemented Agile development methodologies to streamline project processes and enhance team collaboration.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The "IDF School of Computer Science," also known as "Basmach," is a program within the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) that provides advanced training and education in computer science and related fields to soldiers who have been identified as having exceptional talent in these areas. The program was established in the 1980s as a response to the growing importance of computing and technology in the military.
Soldiers who are accepted into the Basmach program receive intensive training in computer science, software engineering, cybersecurity, and other related fields, as well as practical experience working on real-world military technology projects. The program is highly selective and competitive, and graduates are highly sought after by leading technology companies and startups in Israel and around the world.
The Basmach program is part of a larger trend in the Israeli military of investing in high-tech capabilities and developing the next generation of military technology leaders. Through programs like Basmach, the IDF aims to stay at the forefront of technological innovation and maintain its strategic military advantage in a rapidly changing global security environment.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Academic excellence and contribution to the local community have been at the heart of the College's vision since its establishment. The Academic College of Tel Aviv-Yafo was founded in 1994 as a non-profit public institution, and in under three decades has made substantial progress in realizing its vision.
 
The College's six schools offer nine Bachelors programs and ten Masters programs. 4,780 students are currently enrolled in the College, which already boasts nearly 19,000 graduates. Our alumni have amassed significant achievements in academia and business, as well as civic activities and public initiatives. 
 
The schools of Management & Economics, Computer Sciences, Behavioral Sciences, Nursing Sciences, Government & Society, and Information Systems offer students a range of study programs that encourage inspiring and collaborative dialog among various disciplines.
 
The College’s curricula are rigorous and challenging, and the faculty is committed to high quality teaching and active, ongoing research. The College's "open door" policy helps students cope with both the demanding study requirements as well as with personal problems.

companyResolutionResult.description:
Latent is a boutique engineering firm focused on AI. Latent delivers quality in a timely fashion. By being product orientated Latent is able to work with minimal guidance by translating your KPIs into technical specifications. This allows customers to work without needing to first acquire deep knowledge of AI and instead focusing on sharing KPIs and domain expertise.

Latent Services:
- Hands on development - Developing AI based applications.
- Consulting - We use knowledge gained from hands-on experience to give companies guidance on existing or future projects.

We develop a wide range of applications such as Custom model, Chat, Co-pilot, Search, Ticket resolution, image generation all while using the latest best practices.
We serve a wide array of customers from banks to startups. Some of our customers: Google, Thales Security, Marble, Discount bank, Bliss, IDF, ANU, Prime ministers office, Roundforest.

www.reachlatent.com

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The "IDF School of Computer Science," also known as "Basmach," is a program within the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) that provides advanced training and education in computer science and related fields to soldiers who have been identified as having exceptional talent in these areas. The program was established in the 1980s as a response to the growing importance of computing and technology in the military.
Soldiers who are accepted into the Basmach program receive intensive training in computer science, software engineering, cybersecurity, and other related fields, as well as practical experience working on real-world military technology projects. The program is highly selective and competitive, and graduates are highly sought after by leading technology companies and startups in Israel and around the world.
The Basmach program is part of a larger trend in the Israeli military of investing in high-tech capabilities and developing the next generation of military technology leaders. Through programs like Basmach, the IDF aims to stay at the forefront of technological innovation and maintain its strategic military advantage in a rapidly changing global security environment.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Basmach - IDF School of Computer Science

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

companyResolutionResult.description:
CrowdStrike (Nasdaq: CRWD), a global cybersecurity leader, has redefined modern security with the world’s most advanced cloud-native platform for protecting critical areas of enterprise risk — endpoints and cloud workloads, identity and data.

Powered by the CrowdStrike Security Cloud and world-class AI, the CrowdStrike Falcon® platform leverages real-time indicators of attack, threat intelligence, evolving adversary tradecraft and enriched telemetry from across the enterprise to deliver hyper-accurate detections, automated protection and remediation, elite threat hunting and prioritized observability of vulnerabilities.

Purpose-built in the cloud with a single lightweight-agent architecture, the Falcon platform delivers rapid and scalable deployment, superior protection and performance, reduced complexity and immediate time-to-value.

CrowdStrike: We stop breaches.
companyResolutionResult.name: CrowdStrike

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev

companyResolutionResult.description:
Robotican: Pioneering Autonomous Robotics for a Smarter Future

With over a decade of innovation, Robotican stands as a recognized leader in developing and manufacturing cutting-edge robotics technologies and autonomous systems solutions. We are at the forefront of shaping the future of unmanned ground vehicles (UGV), drones (UAV), and advanced robotic applications.

Our latest breakthrough redefines robotics R&D and AI training: a sophisticated digital twin platform. This cost-effective, virtual sandbox accurately emulates real-world mechanical behavior, weather conditions, and topography, providing an unparalleled environment for machine learning and rigorous testing of our autonomous drones, robots, and vehicles. This innovation significantly accelerates development cycles and ensures robust, reliable performance.

Robotican's core focus areas include indoor hybrid drones, advanced autonomous drone interceptors, and modular robotics kits that empower a wide range of robots with cutting-edge autonomous capabilities. We offer powerful, modular robotics systems specially designed for research applications, leveraging our deep expertise in mechatronics and AI robotics to build innovative, high-performance solutions.

Founded in 2013, Robotican is experiencing rapid growth. We are actively expanding into new markets, seeking strategic partnerships, and continuously looking for talented individuals to join our team of robotics engineers and AI specialists as we continue to push the boundaries of autonomous technology. Connect with us to explore how our solutions can transform your operations.
companyResolutionResult.name: ROBOTICAN

Partner is a leading Israeli communications group founded in 1977. Its shares are traded on the Tel-Aviv Stock Exchange. The company provides communications services to more than 3 million customers, including 5G cellular through the brands Partner 5G and 012 Mobile, fixed-line telephony, fiber optic internet through the brand Partner Fiber, internet television through the brand Partner TV, international long-distance calls, end devices and a variety of solutions for businesses. The company also operates in the energy sector as a supplier of electricity to business customers.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
“Shenkar - Engineering. Design. Art” has established itself as one of the leading colleges in Israel. Every year, Shenkar's graduates find themselves in the forefront of research and the industry, exhibit their works, participate in competitions and win prestigious awards.

Combining faculties of engineering, design and art is the basis of this institution’s unique multidisciplinary academics, allowing students and professors to combine cutting-edge technologies with contemporary design and artistic concepts. Each faculty has its own character, yet maintains fruitful collaborations with the other faculties, exposing students to a variety of disciplines and different fields of knowledge.

In the Pernick Faculty of Engineering, 5 departments offer a Bachelor’s Degree in Science (B.Sc.): the Department of Industrial Engineering and Management, the Department of Plastics Engineering, the Department of Software Engineering and the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering. A Master’s Degree program is offered in the Department of Plastics Engineering and the Department of Industrial Engineering and Management, which offers a unique program for those qualified.
In the Azrieli Faculty of Design, 6 departments offer a Bachelor’s Degree in Design (B.Des): The Department of Visual Communications, The Department of Textile Design, the Department of Interior - Building and Environment Design, the Department of Jewelry Design, the Department of Industrial Design and the Department of Fashion Design, considered one of the best of its kind worldwide.

In 2008, a new department was established, the Department of Multidisciplinary Art, which offers a Bachelor’s Degree in Fine Arts (B.F.A), and later, the unique program for a Master’s Degree in Design was introduced (M.Des), offering either a thesis or non-thesis routes for a degree in Fashion, Textile, Jewelry and Accessories Design.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Shenkar - Engineering. Design. Art.

We encourage our students to dream big and follow their passions.

companyResolutionResult.description:
At Linnovate, we lead the way in open-source innovation, driving digital transformation for organizations worldwide. Since our establishment in 2006, we’ve been devoted to delivering forward-thinking software solutions tailored to the unique needs of our customers.

Our core philosophy is that open-source isn't just about technology; it's about empowerment. By harnessing this collaborative spirit, we provide organizations with the products and services they need to effectively navigate the complexities of the modern digital landscape.

Whether we're developing secure enterprise applications or providing expert DevOps services, our expertise spans a wide array of open-source technologies. This ensures that our customers have access to the most cutting-edge solutions available, along with 24/7 enterprise-grade support.

Transparency, responsibility, trust, and integrity shape our approach at Linnovate. We actively contribute to open-source communities and embrace the latest advancements, driving innovation and fostering long-term partnerships.

We believe every team member plays a significant role in driving our business forward, and we encourage a creative mindset to fuel our enterprises. With a growth mindset, we continuously seek to improve, innovate, and deliver solutions with impact for our customers.

resumePosition.details:
• Developed firmware for optical converter systems, customized for diverse client requirements.
• Evaluated, integrated, and validated new commercial modules (Wiznet, Moxa), ensuring seamless HW/SW interoperability.
• Implemented communication protocol support (TCP/IP, SNMP), reducing system complexity and establishing reusable, scalable components for future platforms.
• Designed and built a real-time Command & Control system for an RF-to-Optics converter used by defense companies.
• Developed embedded C firmware on ARM Cortex-M architecture, implementing peripheral drivers through direct MMIO register access (GPIO, timers, I2C, SPI, UART, RS-232).
• Integrated the system with a C# GUI over TCP/IP.
• Implemented full board-management capabilities, enabling remote monitoring, configuration, and firmware updates via a custom UART/TCP bootloader - significantly improving field diagnostics, and overall operational efficiency.
• Performed root-cause analysis and troubleshooting on production embedded systems, diagnosing both software defects and hardware-level faults.
• Utilized JTAG, GDB, oscilloscope, multimeter, and OPM tools to isolate issues, delivering firmware patches and hardware improvements to optimize system behavior.
• Implemented fixes that reduced field failures and enhanced long-term system reliability.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
HIT – Holon Institute of Technology was established in 1969 and became an independent public academic institution of higher education in 1999, certified by the Council of Higher Education. 

HIT focuses on the teaching of sciences, engineering, computer science and technology, management of technology and design. It also emphasizes multi-disciplinary theoretical and practical research of innovative technologies from a professional scientific, economic and cultural perspective.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: ORT Colleges

companyResolutionResult.description:
Opsys has developed the world's most advanced pure solid-state scanning LiDAR. Opsys technology uses fully addressable VCSEL arrays, combined with a single chip addressable CMOS SPAD array. No moving parts allows for a reliable solution that can operate 24/7. This ensures there are no mechanical failures over time, which increases the LiDAR's lifetime expectancy well beyond any other solution on the market today. 

The patented technology provides a 4D point cloud in real-time. Opsys LiDAR provides major cost savings as it is a semiconductor-based solution.

Opsys’ ALTOS is a high-performance smart LiDAR designed as a fully integrated solution to meet the myriads of demands in industry. The ALTOS (Advanced LiDAR Technology Opsys Sensor) is already in mass production. 

Opsys’ smart LiDAR supports a PoE based solution, which offers several benefits, particularly in environments where simplicity, cost efficiency, and flexibility are key, for example, in Intelligent Traffic Systems (ITS)/Smart Cities, Security and Smart Factories. 

All Opsys products are NEMA TS2 certified and NDAA compliant. 
  
Founded in 2016, the company has major investment, is headquartered in Israel, with offices in USA, APAC, and Europe.
companyResolutionResult.name: Opsys Technologies
